{"step_by_step_thinking": "Hyaline membrane disease, also known as respiratory distress syndrome (RDS), primarily affects newborns, particularly premature infants. In these infants, the lungs lack sufficient surfactant, leading to alveolar collapse during expiration. This results in a smaller functional residual capacity (FRC) because the lungs cannot maintain adequate air volume in the alveoli. The closing volume, on the other hand, is the volume at which the smallest airways begin to close. In RDS, the FRC is smaller than the closing volume due to the increased tendency for alveolar collapse.", "answer_choice": "A"}
